name: Cerro de los Yegüeros to Río Guardal from Benamaurel - circular via Geoparque de Granada
name_original: Cerro de los Yegüeros a Río Guardal desde Benamaurel — circular por Geoparque de Granada
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Benamaurel traverse a diverse landscape in Spain's Granada Province, characterized by arid terrain, unique geological formations, and a network of gullies and gorges. The region, part of the Granada Geopark, features contrasting scenery from hostile mountains to lush valleys with almond and olive groves. Riders can explore areas near the scenic Negratín Lake and the unique cave dwellings carved into the rock faces. This varied topography provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Benamaurel The most popular mountain bike trail is Negratín Reservoir Loop from Zújar, a 44.4 miles (71.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding arid landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ojo de la Heredad — circular desde Zújar por Cerro Jabalcón, a moderate 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route provides panoramic vistas from Cerro Jabalcón, overlooking the unique geological formations of the area. Local mountain bikers also love the Granada Geopark: Castilléjar, Huéscar, and Galera Loop, a 26.3 miles (42.3 km) trail leading through the Granada Geopark, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. This route showcases the distinctive desert-like environments and ancient cave dwellings of the region. Mountain biking around Benamaurel is defined by arid landscapes, unique geological formations, and the presence of Negratín Lake. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging difficult routes. The routes in Benamaurel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Benamaurel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Benamaurel?
A: Benamaurel offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with over 35 routes available to explore. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, from arid terrain to lush valleys, providing varied experiences for riders.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Benamaurel?
A: The trails around Benamaurel cater to various skill levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone, from casual riders to experienced mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Benamaurel?
A: Yes, Benamaurel features several excellent circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035401759/ojo-de-la-heredad-circular-desde-zujar-por-cerro-jabalcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de la Heredad — circular desde Zújar por Cerro Jabalcón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.1 km path offering panoramic vistas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143747353/cerro-de-los-yegueeros-to-rio-guardal-from-benamaurel-circular-via-geoparque-de-granada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de los Yegüeros to Río Guardal from Benamaurel - circular via Geoparque de Granada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 25 km route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Benamaurel?
A: Benamaurel's mountain bike trails are characterized by a diverse and often arid landscape, typical of the Granada Geopark. You'll encounter unique geological formations, gullies, and gorges. The terrain can range from challenging unpaved segments to smoother paths, with striking contrasts between hostile mountains and valleys filled with almond and olive groves.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes offer views of the scenic Negratín Lake, and you can explore areas with unique cave dwellings carved into rock faces. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de la Heredad&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter ancient Arabic irrigation systems and historical mills.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by Negratín Lake?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/negratin-reservoir-loop-from-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Loop from Zújar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.4 km trail that offers extensive views of the reservoir and its surrounding arid landscapes. The lake area also features highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Baths of Negratín&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6585835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zújar Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Benamaurel?
A: The mountain bike trails in Benamaurel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the unique arid landscapes, the diverse geological formations, and the opportunity to explore the Granada Geopark and areas around Negratín Lake.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Benamaurel?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the presence of 5 easy routes suggests options suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes often feature paved or less challenging surfaces, making them accessible for a wider range of riders.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Benamaurel?
A: Benamaurel's climate, characterized by arid conditions, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the weather is more stable, providing comfortable conditions for exploring the trails. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler temperatures, though riding is often still possible.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Granada Geopark?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206217419/granada-geopark-castillejar-huescar-and-galera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark: Castilléjar, Huéscar, and Galera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42.3 km trail specifically designed to showcase the distinctive desert-like environments and ancient cave dwellings within the Geopark.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: For those seeking a challenge with significant elevation, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143313121/negratin-reservoir-loop-from-zujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Loop from Zújar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 1350 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to viewpoints in the Benamaurel area?
A: Yes, many routes offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035401759/ojo-de-la-heredad-circular-desde-zujar-por-cerro-jabalcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de la Heredad — circular desde Zújar por Cerro Jabalcón&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas from Cerro Jabalcón. Additionally, specific highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from Horro Mano de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guard Hut Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are accessible and offer stunning scenery.

This cemetery has a long and controversial history and many graves were left in ruins. However, it is considered by some as a patrimonial attraction to take care of because here is the history of the last 200 years of Benamaurel. It is currently closed to the public as efforts are being made to clean it up and even make it a point of interest with an explanation of its history.
